articles.title: Trump’s Pursuit of a Partnership With China Raises Concerns in India
articles.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio has a “gargantuan task” during his visit to Delhi: defuse tensions over President Trump’s anti-India aggression and overtures to China.

articles.title: Coal mine gas explosion in China kills 90 people, state media say
articles.summary: A gas explosion at a coal mine in China's northern province of Shanxi killed at least 90 people. Official news agency Xinhua said the accident happened on Friday evening. Around 247 workers were on du

articles.title: The aftermath of Trump-Xi summit: comparing U.S. and China announcements
articles.summary: Analysts say a comparison of the readouts issued by the U.S. and China reveals "minor inconsistencies" on issues such as agriculture, tariffs and rare earths. But, experts argue, those differences are

articles.title: Summer electric bills sizzle as the cost of cooling climbs
articles.summary: The temperature is climbing, and so are people's utility bills. Rising electricity prices and hotter-than-usual weather could make it especially costly to stay cool this summer.

articles.title: Chile's MAGA-inspired border control
articles.summary: Chile digs desert trenches along its northern border as President José Antonio Kast pushes a hardline migration crackdown critics say may have little effect.

articles.title: Trump, Defiant After Bad Week, Pushes Ahead on Politically Unpopular Ideas
articles.summary: President Trump continues to act like he’s politically all-powerful, even in the face of indications that he is not.

articles.title: Food, Flashlights and Fans: Floridians Step Up Aid to Cuban Relatives
articles.summary: Months into the oil blockade imposed by President Trump, many Cubans are relying more than ever on relatives in the U.S. for help meeting their daily needs.

articles.title: Short Naps, Long Hours: How Autism Clinics Squeeze Medicaid Dollars Out of Preschoolers
articles.summary: The industry has grown rapidly, straining state budgets. A focus on finances has led to overbilling, fraud and even harm.

articles.title: How $6 Gas Prices Are Affecting the Lives of Californians
articles.summary: California is the nation’s most expensive state for gasoline. The latest spike has forced more residents to change their habits.

articles.title: One solution for Maine's struggling fishing industry? Give fillets away for free
articles.summary: Surging food costs and fuel prices are pummeling Maine's struggling groundfishing industry. But a pandemic-era program is helping to keep it afloat as inflation worsens.

articles.title: 15 years since a deadly tornado brought Joplin, Mo. together, kindness carries on
articles.summary: Nearly 100,000 volunteers helped the town rebuild and a spirit of community service continues to this day. Researchers studying human behavior catastrophes can bring out compassion in surprising ways.

articles.title: 'I'd wait forever, but 334 days is crazy.' USS Ford finally comes home
articles.summary: The USS Ford came home to a hero's welcome. Sailors had been away from home for nearly a year, through two conflicts, a fire and problems with the sewage system.

articles.title: The Risks of Iran’s Threat to Control the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Iran is trying to assert its control over the strait by charging for passage. Experts say it is unlikely to happen, but the threat has unsettled the shipping industry.

articles.title: Once Trump’s Co-Pilot Against Iran, Netanyahu Is Now a Mere Passenger
articles.summary: A partner in the war, Israel has been largely left out of the peace talks, a humbling setback for its prime minister with significant risks for the country.

articles.title: SpaceX launches its biggest, most beefed-up Starship yet on a test flight
articles.summary: The mega rocket made its debut two days after SpaceX CEO Elon Musk announced he's taking the company public. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman said Starship is now one step closer to the moon.

articles.title: Trump administration to force foreigners in the U.S. to apply for a green card abroad
articles.summary: Foreigners in the U.S. who want a green card will need to leave and apply in their home country, the Trump administration announced Friday, in a surprise change to a longstanding policy.

articles.title: Rubio arrives in India ahead of Quad talks as U.S. tries to reset strained ties
articles.summary: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio arrived in India on Saturday ahead of a meeting next week with his counterparts from India, Australia, and Japan, members of the Indo-Pacific strategic alliance.

articles.title: Trump Weighs His Options in Carrying Out New Strikes in Iran
articles.summary: There is no shortage of targets if he decides to strike: Energy facilities left untouched, the deep underground nuclear storage site at Isfahan and missile sites that appear to have been dug out.

articles.title: Hunger grows in Gaza as food aid is cut due to higher costs caused by war in Iran
articles.summary: The U.S.-Israeli war against Iran is driving up the cost of food, leading to funding shortfalls and higher expenses for critical food aid in Gaza.

articles.title: Italy's prime minister wants Israel to apologize for treatment of flotilla activists
articles.summary: Italy's Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni wants an apology from Israel over the treatment of Italian citizens on a flotilla bound for Gaza.

articles.title: UN gravely concerned by an Afghan Taliban law that has provisions on child marriage
articles.summary: The United Nations expressed "grave concern" on Thursday about a new law issued by Afghanistan's Taliban government on separation in marriage which includes provisions on child marriage.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
